My extended family is doing a big reunion trip to Greece. 10 people total: me and my wife, my parents (both mid-60s, my dad uses a cane so accessibility matters), my sister and her husband, my brother, and three teenagers (15, 16, and 17). We've already booked all our flights into and out of Athens so please DO NOT search for flights or plan any air travel -- that's fully handled. We arrive October 12 and our return flights are October 26, so about 2 weeks. We're thinking Athens for a few days, then Santorini, and maybe Crete. We're not super budget but we're also not blowing money -- think comfortable mid-range for 10 people. Help us figure out where to go and how to route this.

OK I like the general shape but two things changed. First, my cousin just got back from Milos and said it was way better than Crete -- less touristy, gorgeous beaches, more chill. So let's swap Crete for Milos. Second, my mom just told me she has a work thing and can only do 12 days instead of 14. So we need to land on October 14 instead of the 12th -- well, we'll still fly in the 12th but she arrives the 14th and everyone else will just have 2 extra days in Athens at the start. Adjust the main group itinerary to 12 days starting October 14 and work Milos in instead of Crete.

Let's do hotels now. We need 4-5 rooms at each stop -- my parents need their own room with ground floor or elevator access, each couple gets a room, my brother gets his own, and the three teens can share one room. Budget is $150-200 per night per room. BUT for Santorini specifically, I'd rather rent one big villa where we can all stay together instead of separate hotel rooms -- something with a pool and a view if possible, up to like $800-1000/night total for the whole group. Search for both hotel options for Athens and Milos and a vacation rental or villa for Santorini.

Now plan our days. Keep in mind: my dad can't do steep hikes or long walks, so nothing too physically demanding for the whole group. The teenagers are going crazy wanting water sports -- kayaking, snorkeling, maybe cliff jumping on Milos. My mom specifically asked for a cooking class and wants to go to local markets wherever we are. I personally want at least 2 full days that are completely free -- no schedule, just beach and whatever happens. And sunset spots are non-negotiable for my mom, she's obsessed. Find us the best sunset viewing spots at each stop.

Hmm, this is too packed. I literally said I wanted at least 2 completely free days and you've scheduled something on every single day. Also I don't see any water sports or adventure activities for the teens -- they're going to be miserable if every day is temples and walking tours. And where are the sunset spots I asked for? My mom will absolutely notice if that's missing. Scale it back, add real free days with nothing planned, get the teens some action, and make sure every island has a sunset plan.

Now let's figure out food. My sister is vegetarian and her husband is strictly gluten-free, so every restaurant needs to work for them. Everyone else eats everything. We need one really special group dinner -- it's my parents' 40th wedding anniversary on October 20th and I want to surprise them. Find somewhere with private dining or at least a terrace with an incredible view, somewhere that can seat all 10 of us. The rest of the meals should be casual tavernas, nothing fancy. Also the teens eat like bottomless pits so portions matter.

This is looking great. Give me the full trip review -- flag anything we should book in advance, any timing risks, and the complete summary. Also I need help with logistics: what's the best way to get between the islands? Give me ferry options and schedules. Do we need to rent a car on any of the islands or can we get by without one? What about SIM cards for the group -- what's the cheapest way to get data for 10 phones? Should we get travel insurance for a group this size? And once you've got all that, push the whole trip to Wanderlog so we can share it with everyone.
